Power and Thermals — NVIDIA GB200 NVL Multi-Node Tuning Guide

findings: The GB200 NVL72 incorporates pretuned GPU power workload profiles for LLM training and inference that automatically optimize TGP, GPC clocks, and Mem clocks based on customer Max-Q vs Max-P requirements, eliminating manual power tuning.

The GB200 NVL72 implements Power Smoothing to manage large power swings from bulk synchronous workloads that start/stop simultaneously at the data center level, preventing failures at the utility, transformer, and UPS level.
The GB200 NVL72 features Power Balancing/Sloshing, which allows GPUs to efficiently consume unused power from other rack/cluster components, enhancing GPU performance within provisioned power limits.
The GB200 NVL72 provides a new set of power tuning features to achieve the best performance per watt for common workloads such as DL inference and training, reducing application tuning steps.
Power profiles can be configured via BCM, DCGMI, NVSMI, or OOB (Redfish APIs on the HMC), with priority-based selection and automatic conflict resolution.
